title: Fix randomize 'invalid empty bit-vector literal' and ignored `with`
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

```
./test_regress/t/t_randomize_method_with.py 
driver.py: Found 24 cores, using -j 24
driver.py: Found 24 cores, using -j 24
==SUMMARY: Left 1  Passed 0  Failed 0  Time 0:00
======================================================================
vlt/t_randomize_method_with: ==================================================
	export GCOV_PREFIX_STRIP='99'
	export GCOV_PREFIX='/nobackup/loran/sols/misc/verilator/verilator/test_regress/obj_dist/gcov_1'
	perl /nobackup/loran/sols/misc/verilator/verilator/test_regress/../bin/verilator --prefix Vt_randomize_method_with -cc -Mdir obj_vlt/t_randomize_method_with --fdedup --debug-check --comp-limit-members 10 --x-assign unique --make gmake --exe ../obj_vlt/t_randomize_method_with/Vt_randomize_method_with__main.cpp --fno-inline --clk clk -f input.vc +define+TEST_OBJ_DIR=obj_vlt/t_randomize_method_with +define+TEST_DUMPFILE=obj_vlt/t_randomize_method_with/simx.vcd t/t_randomize_method_with.v   > obj_vlt/t_randomize_method_with/vlt_compile.log
%Warning-WIDTHTRUNC: t/t_randomize_method_with.v:80:6: Logical operator ASSERT expects 1 bit on the Property, but Property's FUNCREF 'randomize' generates 32 bits.
                                                     : ... note: In instance 'mwith'
   80 |      assert(randomize(u) with {
      |      ^~~~~~
                     ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTHTRUNC?v=5.037
                     ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTHTRUNC */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: t/t_randomize_method_with.v:82:2: Internal: Unexpected Call
   82 |  $countones(u) > 2;
      |  ^~~~~~~~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.037 for more assistance.
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_randomize_method_with.v:82:2: ../V3AstNodeExpr.h:59: Unexpected Call
   82 |  $countones(u) > 2;
      |  ^~~~~~~~~~
                        ... This fatal error may be caused by the earlier error(s); resolve those first.
%Warning: vlt/t_randomize_method_with: Exec of verilator failed: %Warning-WIDTHTRUNC: t/t_randomize_method_with.v:80:6: Logical operator ASSERT expects 1 bit on the Property, but Property's FUNCREF 'randomize' generates 32 bits.
vlt/t_randomize_method_with: %Error: Exec of verilator failed: %Warning-WIDTHTRUNC: t/t_randomize_method_with.v:80:6: Logical operator ASSERT expects 1 bit on the Property, but Property's FUNCREF 'randomize' generates 32 bits.
vlt/t_randomize_method_with: FAILED: Exec of verilator failed: %Warning-WIDTHTRUNC: t/t_randomize_method_with.v:80:6: Logical operator ASSERT expects 1 bit on the Property, but Property's FUNCREF 'randomize' generates 32 bits.
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00

======================================================================
#vlt/t_randomize_method_with: %Error: Exec of verilator failed: %Warning-WIDTHTRUNC: t/t_randomize_method_with.v:80:6: Logical operator ASSERT expects 1 bit on the Property, but Property's FUNCREF 'randomize' generates 32 bits.
		make && test_regress/t/t_randomize_method_with.py  --vlt
==TESTS DONE, FAILED: Passed 0  Failed 1  Time 0:00
```

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained and self-checking so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs

```
git diff
diff --git a/test_regress/t/t_randomize_method_with.v b/test_regress/t/t_randomize_method_with.v
index d6436baf2..08e1dd9d7 100644
--- a/test_regress/t/t_randomize_method_with.v
+++ b/test_regress/t/t_randomize_method_with.v
@@ -75,7 +75,12 @@ endclass
 
 class Baz2;
   rand int v;
+  logic [9:0] u;
   function bit test_this_randomize;
+     assert(randomize(u) with {
+       u[5] == '0;
+       $countones(u) > 2;
+     });
     return this.randomize() with { v == 5; } == 1;
   endfunction
 endclass
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

`./test_regress/t/t_randomize_method_with.py`

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

`Verilator 5.037 devel rev v5.036-189-gcd0f35fe6`

What OS and distribution are you using?

`Linux 5.14.0-570.19.1.el9_6.x86_64`
